mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in FEDFUNDS raises the discount rate on equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, causing lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 1.68 as of the latest available monthly snapshot before 2022-08-15
failure_condition: Reject if rising 3-month FEDFUNDS changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations or the return slope is nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL 12-month inflation crosses above 6%, investors price a higher probability of restrictive central bank policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 295.271 in the latest available snapshot before 2022-08-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered high-inflation observations have average 5-day S&P 500 returns greater than the unconditional average or a negative hit rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE is at or below 3.6% while FEDFUNDS rises more than 75 basis points over 3 months, labor tightness forces additional tightening and lowers S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 3.5 and FEDFUNDS was 1.68 in the latest available monthly snapshot
regime_hint: late-cycle monetary tightening with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not produce lower 20-day returns than either UNRATE or FEDFUNDS alone, or if triggered mean returns are positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained PAYEMS growth with no material UNRATE increase signals resilient household income and supports earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises for 3 consecutive months and UNRATE 3-month change <= 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 152536 and UNRATE was 3.5 in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the PAYEMS-UNRATE interaction adds no predictive power versus PAYEMS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and future construction activity, leading to lower S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 1559.0 in the latest available snapshot before 2022-08-15
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST declines below the trigger are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or the conditional mean is not below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in INDPRO indicates improving real output and capacity utilization, supporting earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO was 104.3648 in the latest available snapshot before 2022-08-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO trends have nonpositive average 20-day S&P 500 returns or a rolling 3-year Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth fails to keep pace with CPIAUCSL growth over 3 months, real consumer demand is contracting and earnings expectations fall, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth minus CPIAUCSL 3-month growth crosses below 0
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS was 680591.0 and CPIAUCSL was 295.271 in the latest available snapshots
failure_condition: Reject if triggered real-consumption-squeeze periods do not underperform baseline 60-day returns by at least 1 percentage point or have positive mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: Negative GDPC1 growth combined with a still-low UNRATE marks an early recession-recognition phase in which earnings downgrades lag activity weakness, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter growth crosses below 0 and UNRATE <= 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 was 19681.682 and UNRATE was 3.5 in the latest available snapshots
regime_hint: early-cycle downturn with lagging labor deterioration
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1 contraction and low-UNRATE trigger produces positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or is not worse than low UNRATE alone.
